mechanism_narrative: The 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS is scored continuously; falling or pinned policy rates reduce equity discount rates and increase risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.12 percent in the latest available October 2009 snapshot
regime_hint: monetary easing or zero-rate policy regime
failure_condition: Reject if lower 3-month FEDFUNDS changes do not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than higher FEDFUNDS changes in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating household income expectations and credit stress; when unemployment crosses upward rapidly, equity earnings expectations fall and S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 10.2 percent in the latest available October 2009 snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and HOUST signals that consumer demand and residential investment are recovering together; this broad demand rebound raises expected earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change crosses above 0 and HOUST 3-month change crosses above 0
evidence_refs: fact:latest available September 2009 values were RSAFS 344688 and HOUST 590
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive RSAFS and HOUST triggers fail to outperform single-series triggers over 60 trading days, or if triggered positive-return frequency is below 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO trend indicates that firms are increasing output and capacity utilization, which improves operating leverage and cyclical earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available September 2009 INDPRO vintage value was 98.4622
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum is not associated with higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative momentum in at least 60 percent of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero, markets price future policy tightening and margin pressure; this inflation-at-zero-rate interaction produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change crosses above 3 percent while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 percent
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS was 0.12 percent and CPIAUCSL was 215.791
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration with FEDFUNDS below 0.5 percent produces nonnegative 60-day excess returns relative to baseline, or if interaction terms add no predictive power versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A GDPC1 quarterly growth rate crossing from negative to positive marks a transition from contraction to expansion; the expected earnings trough is revised upward and S&P 500 returns are positive over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth crosses above 0 percent
evidence_refs: fact:latest available GDPC1 vintage observation was 13014.0 for 2009-07-01
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth crossovers above zero are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55 percent of cases, or if average triggered returns are below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS losses moderate while UNRATE remains elevated, equity markets treat the labor market as near a cyclical trough; reduced recession tail risk produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change improves versus the prior 3-month change while UNRATE is above 8 percent
evidence_refs: fact:latest available October 2009 values were PAYEMS 130848 and UNRATE 10.2
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS improvement conditional on UNRATE above 8 percent has no higher 20-day return than PAYEMS improvement when UNRATE is below 8 percent, or if conditional hit rate is below 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in RSAFS signals an immediate consumer-spending shock; because consumption weakness directly lowers near-term revenue expectations, S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth crosses below -1 percent
evidence_refs: fact:latest available September 2009 RSAFS vintage value was 344688
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS monthly declines below -1 percent are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of triggered observations, or if downside response is not statistically different from baseline.
